one_line_definition: Ultra-porous amorphous silica network formed by sol-gel processing and supercritical drying, with very high porosity and low density
Resistance of a material to uniform isotropic compression, K = -V(∂P/∂V)_T
MD simulation where groups of atoms are represented as single interaction sites, enabling access to larger length/time scales
Exponent describing how mass scales with length in a self-similar porous network; aerogels typically show Df ≈ 1.7–1.8
Wet-chemistry route where silica monomers hydrolyse and condense into a network gel, used to synthesise aerogels and other porous oxides
Number of interparticle bonds per unit volume in a coarse-grained gel model; found to be the primary correlate of bulk modulus
Empirical relationship between modulus and density in aerogels, with exponent m ≈ 3–3.5
Statistical distribution of pore diameters in a porous material, characterised here via geometric sphere-insertion algorithm
Variation in primary particle size in a gel model; increases softness by reducing bond density
Equation-of-motion integrator including random force and drag terms to model solvent effects without explicit solvent molecules
Self-ordered arrays of water droplets that form on an evaporatively cooled liquid surface in moist air, used as templates for porous films
Fluid motion driven by surface-tension gradients arising from temperature non-uniformity; responsible for stabilising breath-figure droplets against coalescence
Polymer film containing a regular 2D or 3D array of micrometer-scale pores formed by breath-figure templating
Close-packing arrangement of spheres or droplets in a plane, giving maximum 2D packing density; observed in breath-figure films
Frequency range in which light propagation is forbidden in a periodic dielectric structure; macroporous films with sub-micron pores can exhibit this
Science and practice of measuring and characterising the geometric form and texture of surfaces at micro/nano scales
Instrument that drags a diamond-tipped stylus across a surface to measure its height profile
Optical method using white-light fringes to map surface height with nm vertical resolution over mm lateral range
Optical technique that uses a pinhole or Nipkow disk to reject out-of-focus light, enabling depth-resolved surface imaging
Scanning probe technique using a cantilever tip to measure surface forces/topography at nm resolution on any material
Linear phase-preserving filter that separates roughness from waviness by suppressing wavelengths above a cutoff; standardised in ISO 11562
Variant of the Gaussian filter using iterative weighting to suppress influence of outliers (scratches, freak peaks) on the mean line
Surface produced by two or more manufacturing steps with different characteristic wavelengths, e.g., coarse + fine honing of cylinder liners

aliases_seen: material ratio curve; bearing area curve
one_line_definition: Cumulative height distribution of a surface profile used to characterise bearing area and oil-retention capacity
ISO 13565-2 parameters describing core roughness depth, reduced peak height, reduced valley depth, and material ratios of a plateau surface

aliases_seen: laser surface texturing; LST; dimple texturing
one_line_definition: Deliberate creation of regular micro-features (dimples, grooves) on a surface to improve tribological performance via micro-hydrodynamic bearing or lubricant reservoir effects
Surface with a deterministic, designed pattern (steps, dimples, channels) intended to fulfil specific functional requirements
Signal processing technique that decomposes surface topography into components at multiple length scales simultaneously
Lateral spacing between measurement points in a surface profile or map; must satisfy Nyquist criterion relative to surface bandwidth
